published_prior_art.cite: DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024, Theorem 1: sharp causal bounds for a discrete structural model with user-supplied assumptions and evidence are exactly its polynomial-program optima.
MiaoGengTchetgen2018 supplies the known-valid-proxy setting; this statement is the proposal's definitional oracle sanity reduction.

DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 supplies polynomial-program dual/branch-and-bound machinery; generic Positivstellensatz theory supplies finite infeasibility certificates, but not the requested proxy-model-specific useful degree analysis.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The asserted finite union sharpness is a direct finite enumeration/application of Duarte et al.'s general sharp polynomial-program theorem, not a new sharpness result.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1 gap and novelty justification
novelty_flags.one_line: They characterize Duarte et al. as limited to a “fixed discrete SCM” and unable to establish this sharpness, although its theorem covers a user-specified discrete structural model with additional assumptions and empirical evidence and returns its sharp polynomial-program bounds.

soundness_flags.label: target_estimand, def:component-ate, and Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The core declares only that Y has values in an arbitrary finite set, but uses y1-y0 and E[Y(1)-Y(0)] without requiring Y⊆R or defining a numerical outcome score.

soundness_flags.label: ass:lower-bound-validity and def:component-fiber
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed fortified valid-proxy model omits latent treatment positivity P(A=a|U,Cα*)>0 required by the cited validity definition; strict observed-cell positivity does not imply it, so the stated fiber is not the cited union model.

soundness_flags.label: ass:strict-observed-positivity
soundness_flags.one_line: Full positivity of every observed Y,A,W,Z cell is unused by the response-function/polynomial formulation and unnecessarily excludes boundary laws that the stated conditional-independence polynomials can represent.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of the American Statistical Association 119(547), 2024, pp. 1778–1793
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper rewrites a discrete structural equation model, additional modeling assumptions, and empirical evidence as a polynomial program, and Theorem 1 states that its extrema are sharp causal bounds.

A valid subset requires latent ignorability, latent-stratum treatment positivity, and the two proxy restrictions; the unknown valid set then satisfies |ν*|≥γ and defines a union of viable causal models.

source_verification_receipts.locator: journal summary; categorical rank formulation corroborated by the corresponding author version, Section 2, condition (i)
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper identifies effects with two proxies under a rank/invertibility condition, rather than furnishing the proposed unknown-valid-subset union sharpness theorem.
The paper studies identification and inference with nonunique bridge solutions and gives regular root-n inference under additional conditions.
The paper develops proxy partial-identification bounds without completeness and smooth approximations for inference.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of the American Statistical Association 92(439), 1997, pp. 1171–1176
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper gives tight nonparametric treatment-effect bounds in the imperfect-compliance model, the response-type sharp-bounds precedent.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Pivot from finite enumeration to a genuinely new proxy-specific object: for example, a computable strict tightening or closed-form frontier that exploits fortified-proxy structure and demonstrably improves on generic Autobounds output.

If retaining a finite-SCM result, make its contribution algorithmic: give a single encoded union model, explicit input size and a termination/complexity or certificate-degree theorem that is stronger than applying general polynomial optimization componentwise.

Repair the model before any redevelopment: require a numeric outcome support/score, state latent treatment positivity if “valid” follows Yu et al., and explicitly choose either a semi-Markov model allowing arbitrary P(U,Cα) or a DAG factorization consistent with the response-type fiber.
Drop strict full observed-cell positivity unless a specific certificate theorem needs it; state the weaker condition precisely and prove its role.

recommended_next_step: Pivot the central kernel; do not advance this finite-union sharpness formulation to Stage 0 because its novelty is subsumed by the published general discrete-SCM polynomial-program theorem.
